visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern, support-center layout with a corporate feel. Dark top bar with a prominent search field, light content area, and card-based article layout. Visual style is minimalist with restrained use of color and emphasis on typography and structure over decoration.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by contrast, size, and layout. The dark navy header contrasts strongly with the light page background, making the search area the initial focal point. The main H1 ('How to connect your CommerceHQ store') is the largest text element and dark navy, creating the primary focus in the content area. Within the main article card, bold section titles and numbered steps guide scanning. The right-hand 'Related Articles' card is secondary but visually distinct through its card framing and orange link text.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous whitespace around the main content column and between the two primary cards. Cards have ample internal padding, with clear separation between headings, paragraphs, and lists. The header-to-content transition has significant vertical spacing, creating breathing room and emphasizing the top-level sections.
unique_elements: Large, full-width search bar centered within a dark header band, acting as the primary interaction entry point.
Two-column article layout with a dominant main content card and a narrower right sidebar card for related articles.
Breadcrumb trail above the H1 with color-differentiated 'FAQ' label in orange to indicate category.
Numbered step list where numbers and some inline terms are visually emphasized (likely via bold or accent color) to aid scannability in procedural content.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Likely a 12-column responsive grid with a centered max-width container. On desktop, main article spans roughly 8 columns and the related-articles sidebar spans 4 columns.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Standard knowledge-base pattern: fixed top header with search, followed by a single main content area that splits into a primary article column and a secondary sidebar column.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top-to-bottom structure: (1) dark header with brand name and full-width search bar, (2) breadcrumb line, (3) large page title, (4) two-column content section with main article card on the left and related-articles card on the right.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned content within a centered page container. Text is left-aligned. Cards align along a common top baseline. Breadcrumb, title, and cards form a clean vertical alignment on the left edge.
responsive_hints: Two-column card layout suggests a breakpoint where the sidebar likely stacks under the main article on smaller viewports.
Full-width search bar within the header indicates it likely shrinks to fit viewport width while keeping generous horizontal padding.
Centered container with large side margins implies fluid width with a max-width constraint for readability on large screens.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. First a full-width dark header contains the brand name at the left and a horizontally centered, long search bar. Below, on the light background, a single breadcrumb row spans the width. Next is a full-width H1 title. Under the title, the content region splits into two columns: a larger left card containing the article body (intro text, then a subheading 'Step-by-Step Guide:' followed by a vertically stacked numbered list), and a narrower right card containing a 'Related Articles' heading with a vertical list of text links.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The dark header occupies the top 15–20% of the viewport height, dominated by the search field. The main content area below uses a centered content column with wide lateral margins (~15–20% of viewport each side on large screens). Horizontally, the left article card takes about two-thirds of the available content width, while the related-articles card takes about one-third. Vertically, the article card consumes the majority of the page height; the related-articles card is noticeably shorter, leaving some whitespace below it.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious. There is clear separation between structural elements (header, breadcrumb, title, cards). Within cards, text is reasonably compact but not cramped, with comfortable line spacing and paragraph spacing. Lists are single-column and easy to scan, making the interface feel readable without being sparse.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye initially lands on the dark header and the large, high-contrast search bar (top center). From there, it moves down to the breadcrumb (left), then to the large H1 heading. After reading the heading, the eye naturally follows a left-to-right flow across the two cards, prioritizing the larger left article card and occasionally glancing to the right sidebar. Within the article, the vertical progression through intro text and numbered steps leads to a strong top-down reading path.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Explicit buttons are not clearly visible in the screenshot, but from the search bar and link styling we can infer a standard flat, modern style: likely rounded rectangles with 4–6px border radius, solid accent (orange) or neutral (navy) background, white text, and no heavy shadows. Minimal or no border, relying on color blocks and hover states for affordance.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Buttons are likely implemented as .

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are white rectangles with subtle rounded corners (approximately 4–6px radius). They have a very light grey border (#E5E7EB) and possibly a minimal, soft drop shadow or border-only separation. Internal padding appears generous (~24–32px). Content inside is strictly vertical: title, then body text or list, with consistent spacing between these elements.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Typical card HTML structure: a wrapper
containing a header and body area, e.g.,

...

...
. The related-articles card contains a heading followed by a vertical list of links; the article card contains heading + paragraphs + lists.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The main visible form element is the search field. It is a full-width, horizontally elongated input with a medium border radius (~6–8px), light grey background (#9CA3AF with opacity or #E5E7EB), and subtle inner padding (~12–16px vertically and 16–24px horizontally). Placeholder text is mid-grey, indicating non-active state. Border is either very subtle or achieved via background contrast rather than a strong line. Labels for other fields (not shown) are likely above-field labels, following the simple, stacked help-center convention.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Forms likely use stacked, full-width inputs with labels above each input: <label for='search'>Search</label><input id='search' type='search'>. The search control in the header may be wrapped in a <form> with a single input and possibly a hidden submit button for keyboard-triggered searches.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Minimalist navigation. At the very top, the brand 'Yakkyofy' acts as a logo/title in the dark bar, left-aligned. There is no visible multi-item navigation menu in the screenshot, emphasizing search-first behavior. Breadcrumb navigation under the header uses small text: the category name 'FAQ' in accent orange followed by a grey divider '/' and the current article title in grey, all left-aligned. Active breadcrumb segment (current page) is plain grey, indicating non-clickable state.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Top brand likely a simple anchor element (Yakkyofy) inside a header. Breadcrumb built as a list or inline links, e.g.,
  1. FAQ
  2. How to connect...
.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Two main list types: (1) procedural numbered list in the article card, where numbers are left-aligned and text flows to the right; numbers may be bold or accent-colored to aid quick scanning. Line spacing is comfortable and list items are not tightly packed. (2) Simple link list in the related-articles card: 2–3 lines of text links, each separated by small vertical spacing (~8–12px). Links are accent orange with no bullet icons, relying on color and placement to convey list behavior.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Numbered steps likely use
  1. for semantic order. Related articles list appears as a vertical list of links, either an unordered list
    • ...
    or simple stacked elements within a container using display:block and margin-bottom for spacing.

heading.visual_style: Bold, modern sans-serif with clear, uniform strokes and relatively high x-height. The H1 is strong and straightforward, prioritizing legibility over personality, typical of a neutral grotesque/humanist mix.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif suitable for longer help articles. Strokes are not overly condensed; counters are open, making paragraphs easy to read.

spacing_system.component_padding: Cards use approximately 24–32px internal padding on all sides. The search bar has around 12–16px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header to breadcrumb, breadcrumb to H1, H1 to cards) appears to follow 24–40px increments, generally 3–5 times the base 8px unit.

interaction_hints: Links in orange likely change shade or become underlined on hover, providing a clear affordance for clickability.
Cards may gain a slightly stronger shadow or subtle background tint on hover to suggest interactivity if sections themselves are clickable.
The search input likely shows a focus ring or border color change (possibly accent orange or blue) when active, with placeholder text dimming and caret in dark color.
Breadcrumb links (except the current page) likely gain underline or color change on hover.
Transitions, if present, are probably subtle and fast (150–250ms) easing on color and shadow changes, matching the clean and professional feel of the help center.
